item.title: Lowering the Old Age Security income threshold is no longer politically taboo
item.description: Canadians are on board with calls to redirect retirement income subsidies to tackle affordability

item.title: How we chose the destinations in our bucket-list trip planner
item.description: Our editors researched incredible itineraries and estimated total costs for flights, hotels, meals and more

item.title: Chart of the Week: Global rates show deeper war fears
item.description: Investors are becoming increasingly hawkish

item.title: Iran fires missiles at UK’s Diego Garcia base
item.description: Attack significantly exceeds Tehran’s previously known capabilities, stoking fears in Europe

item.title: Sir James Dyson tackles rugby
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: the WNBA’s new labour deal and Fifa’s World Cup reliance

item.title: Plan your trip-of-a-lifetime with our handy bucket-list travel guide
item.description: With a sea of hand-picked destinations, our database helps you explore big-ticket vacation ideas for every dream and budget

item.title: Life insurance sales surge in wake of Reeves’ inheritance tax shake-up
item.description: Chancellor’s targeting of pensions prompts more people to take out insurance, say industry figures Tax

item.title: Iran lets grain ships through Hormuz to shore up food supply
item.description: Agriculture has been a ‘pain point’ for Tehran after it imposes de facto blockade of waterway in response to US-Israeli war

item.title: Iran launches 10mn rial banknote as war triggers dash for cash
item.description: Iranians queue to get hold of highest-ever denomination bills as US-Israeli strikes fuel demand for hard currency

item.title: Iran war raises the risk of a bond market shock
item.description: Wild swings in UK gilts point to strains that will hurt government finances and jack up borrowing costs for us all

item.title: EU member states urged to lower gas storage targets due to Iran war
item.description: European Commission proposes steps to try to reduce demand for energy from households and industry

item.title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
item.description: Ride-hailing company must make up lost ground on driverless vehicles, or risk being left in the rear-view mirror

item.title: HMRC ‘unlikely to be lenient’ with tax exiles fleeing Dubai, advisers warn
item.description: Returning Britons could face hit from both income tax and capital gains tax Tax

item.title: Aston Martin could do with a carpool companion
item.description: Brand still has global cachet but it is finding life tricky as a tiny luxury-car maker

item.title: Britain must be more vigilant to the risk of sabotage by hostile states
item.description: If Moscow and Beijing are determined to destabilise the west then new strategies are needed

item.title: Is it ever right to give one child more money than another?
item.description: When it comes to gifts or inheritance, parents must decide whether to give equally, or help those in most need Tax

item.title: Cargill uses AI to get more meat from the bone as beef prices soar
item.description: System called CarVe spots flecks of meat on carcasses as they move along the production line

item.title: P. Louise founder: ‘When you come from the bottom you have nothing to lose’
item.description: Self-taught make-up artist Paige Louise Williams has turned her beauty business into one of the UK’s fastest growing

item.title: How the oil shock could lead to wider economic pain for Canada
item.description: Financial markets are betting that the Bank of Canada will hike interest rates three times this year, starting in July

item.title: Japan’s bullet trains shift to cargo as seats go unfilled
item.description: High-speed rail freight services aim to offset truck driver shortages and dwindling passenger numbers

item.title: Mothers of autistic children in Ontario earn significantly less than fathers, study finds
item.description: Research shows women often shoulder the caretaking responsibilities and their careers are disrupted by the hours spent navigating services for their kids

item.title: Taiwan concerned by depletion of US missile stocks during Iran war
item.description: Some weapons used in Gulf would be crucial in early phases of any conflict with China

item.title: Canadians bought more cannabis, less alcohol in January
item.description: Cannabis industry may be capturing some consumers who are cutting down on alcohol, expert says

item.title: Amazon-backed nuclear reactor group X-energy files for IPO
item.description: Start-up aims to capitalise on rising interest in atomic power due to growing power demand from AI

item.title: Musk defrauded Twitter investors during buyout, jury finds
item.description: Panel members conclude the billionaire misled shareholders in tweets posted during the $44bn takeover

item.title: Canada expanding LNG exports but positioned to fall short of Ottawa’s goals
item.description: While federal government says LNG exports could reach 100 million tonnes per year by 2040, experts forecast Canada will reach roughly half of that

item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Q&A with FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens, on Thursday March 26 at 1pm GMT

item.title: Federal jury finds Musk liable for claims he defrauded Twitter investors before takeover
item.description: Elon Musk accused of trying to drive down stock price in effort to renegotiate or back out of $44-billion deal in 2022

item.title: Big drugmakers saved at least $5bn on US taxes shifting income overseas
item.description: New disclosures show how pharma groups reduced federal levies last year by assigning profits to low-tax jurisdictions Tax

item.title: Flagship Blackstone credit fund posts first monthly loss since 2022
item.description: Loan markdowns and market declines led to negative return for the first time in more than three years

item.title: Supermicro drops 33% after co-founder charged with smuggling Nvidia chips to China
item.description: US prosecutors allege two employees and a contractor conspired to illegally ship servers through south-east Asia

item.title: Goldman lawyer who resigned over Epstein links paid $25mn in 2025
item.description: Kathy Ruemmler to step down as the bank’s general counsel at the end of June

item.title: In Iran war, a shadow looms of $200 oil and a global recession
item.description: A prolonged Gulf standoff could yet deliver a far sharper shock to prices and economic growth

item.title: Middle East war day 21 as it happened: Trump considers ‘winding down’ US military operations, Pentagon orders thousands more troops deployed to region
item.description: President says US ‘very close’ to meeting its military objectives in Iran but does not want a ‘ceasefire’

item.title: Quebec Premier dismisses Doug Ford’s concerns over electric-vehicle mandate
item.description: Ontario Premier had sent letters to Quebec and B.C. asking provinces to drop EV sales targets

item.title: US to deploy thousands more troops to the Middle East
item.description: Marines and sailors to arrive in the region in three to four weeks, signalling possible new phase in conflict

item.description: Middle East war has unleashed severe disruption for the aviation sector

item.title: UK borrowing costs reach highest level since 2008 as economic hit from war mounts
item.description: Ten-year gilt yield jumps to 5% as fears over inflation shock intensify

item.title: A bet on Bill Ackman’s new listing is complex
item.description: The hedge fund manager is seeking to extend his Pershing Square portfolio in multiple vehicles

item.description: Three childhood friends founded a charity in Hamilton and are now fundraising to give their pet sanctuary a permanent home

item.description: Countries with plenty of renewable and nuclear sources are navigating the energy crisis much better

item.description: Sale of Calgary-based supplier to data centre industry will provide a bounty to owners KKR and Mubadala - and a big payout to employees

item.description: An inflationary energy shock has drained optimism over UK rate cuts and hammered popular hedge fund trades

item.description: David Solomon’s comments in annual shareholder letter underscore Wall Street’s wariness around non-bank lending

item.description: Investigation follows failure of Mayfair-based lender and linked companies owned by founder Paresh Raja

item.title: Canadian retail sales up 1.1% to $70.7-billion in January
item.description: Increase led by sales at motor vehicle and parts dealers

item.description: Fourth-quarter outflows in the Americas were $14.1-billion and nearly 200 financial advisers have left the bank over the last year

item.description: Agency’s head Fatih Birol says recovery of oil and gasfields in Gulf region could take more than six months

item.title: Stock markets today: Major indexes end sharply lower, bond yields surge as traders increase bets for BoC and Fed rate hikes

item.title: Federal Reserve holds rates steady, projects single rate cut for 2026
item.description: Fed officials expect Iran war will worsen inflation while having little impact on economic growth
